Flora&Fauna: 2130: At 9:30 the sea became quite agitated and a noise similar to that of tide ripples was heard. The ship's wake had the appearance of the Milky Way, studded with stars of the first magnitude. This Phosphorescence was caused by Mollusem several of which were caught with a bucket.

0630: At 6:30 two water spouts formed upon the lee bow and passed rapidly to leeward. They were distant about 3 miles.

People: 1525: At 15:25 hear the cry of !Man overboard.! Let go the Port life buoy, ordered ropes to be thrown overboard on both sides. Sent boats in search but saw nothing but a Cap in the water. Beat to quarters and found Geo. H. Perkins, Quarter Gunner of 2nd Division was missing. The Boats, after being out until 4 P. M., returned alongside.
0400: Confined John A. Brown in the Sweat Box by order of 1st Lieut.

0100: The temperature and pressure values usually start at 0200 and end at 2400. It looks like the log keeper started copying and putting the first value in the 0100 row, not the 0200 row. I suspect that all these values should be moved one hour down, but I have left them as they were written.
0100: I changed all the baro values like 28.nn to 29.nn.

People: 1810: At 6:10 P. M., James Bray (O.S.), having been confined in the sweat box for fighting, was reported by the Master at Arms as being very disorderly. He was then ordered by the Captain to be taken out of the Sweat Box and put in the Brig, in double irons. The Master at Arms reported him as having broken down both sweat box doors.

Note: 0100: There are no data for 0100. It looks like all the values should be up one row, and a 1200 Knots and Dir should be fudged in, which is what I did..
